2604.26622.title: OCR-Memory: Optical Context Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agent Memory
2604.26622.paper_summary: Autonomous LLM agents increasingly operate in long-horizon, interactive settings where success depends on reusing experience accumulated over extended histories. However, existing agent memory systems are fundamentally constrained by text-context budgets: storing or revisiting raw trajectories is prohibitively token-expensive, while summarization and text-only retrieval trade token savings for information loss and fragmented evidence. To address this limitation, we propose Optical Context Retrieval Memory (OCR-Memory), a memory framework that leverages the visual modality as a high-density representation of agent experience, enabling retention of arbitrarily long histories with minimal prompt overhead at retrieval time. Specifically, OCR-Memory renders historical trajectories into images annotated with unique visual identifiers. OCR-Memory retrieves stored experience via a \emph{locate-and-transcribe} paradigm that selects relevant regions through visual anchors and retrieves the corresponding verbatim text, avoiding free-form generation and reducing hallucination. Experiments on long-horizon agent benchmarks show consistent gains under strict context limits, demonstrating that optical encoding increases effective memory capacity while preserving faithful evidence recovery.
2604.26622.paper_summary_zh: 自主 LLM 代理越來越多地在長期互動環境中運作，成功依賴於重用在長時間歷史中積累的經驗。<lb/>然而，現有的代理記憶系統在文本上下文預算上受到根本限制：儲存或重新訪問原始軌跡的代價過於昂貴，而總結和僅基於文本的檢索則以資訊損失和證據碎片化為代價來節省代幣。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出了光學上下文檢索記憶（OCR-Memory），這是一個利用視覺模態作為代理經驗的高密度表示的記憶框架，能夠在檢索時以最小的提示開銷保留任意長的歷史。<lb/>具體而言，OCR-Memory 將歷史軌跡轉換為帶有獨特視覺標識符的圖像。<lb/>OCR-Memory 通過一種 \emph{定位和轉錄} 的範式檢索儲存的經驗，通過視覺錨點選擇相關區域並檢索相應的逐字文本，避免自由形式生成並減少幻覺。<lb/>在長期代理基準上的實驗顯示，在嚴格的上下文限制下，持續獲得增益，證明光學編碼在保留真實證據恢復的同時增加了有效記憶容量。
2604.26622.author: Jinze Li et.al.
2604.26622.authors: Jinze Li, Yang Zhang, Xin Yang, Jiayi Qu, Jinfeng Xu, Shuo Yang, Junhua Ding, Edith Cheuk-Han Ngai
